LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JAY WIENER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR A VIOLATION OF RIGHT TO PRIVACY, PLAINTIFFS MUST ALLEGE FACTS DESCRIBING THE NATURE AND EXTENT OF THE PUBLIC DISCLOSURE, AND A DESCRIPTION OF THE "DOSSIER." AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, ADDITIONAL FACTS MUST BE ALLEGED TO SUPPORT THE NATURE AND EXTENT OF SEVERE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JAY WIENER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to first cause of action for a violation of right to privacy, plaintiffs must allege facts describing the nature and extent of the public disclosure, and a description of the "dossier." As to the second cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, additional facts must be alleged to support the nature and extent of severe emotional distress. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
